segments.s323-g41: همی is a progressive/habitual marker meaning ‘kept/was continually’. گفت‌گر here is best read as the phrase گفت + گر (the conditional/’if’ particle), i.e. ‘he would say, “if…”’; it marks that what follows is a quoted conditional. تیز is an adverb ‘swift(ly)’. بشتافتم is the past 1st‑person perfective 'I hurried' used inside that conditional, so the whole segment functions as ‘he kept saying, “If I had hurried swiftly…”’.
segments.s323-g42: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. یزدان = the Divine/God. همه modifies کام (here 'every/all'), and کام دل is a genitive phrase 'the heart’s desire(s)'. یافتم is the past 1st‑person 'I obtained/found', completing the counterfactual conditional: 'I would have obtained from God all the heart’s desires.'
segments.s323-g43: بیامد is a simple past 'he came'. The phrase بر تخت شاهی is a locative/goal phrase 'upon the royal throne' that attaches to نشست, and نشست is the past 3rd‑person 'he sat (down)'. The sequence gives two successive past actions: 'he came and sat upon the royal throne.'
segments.s323-g44: یکی here is an indefinite 'a certain' (not the numeral sense). جامه means 'garment/robe'. نابسوده is a poetic negative participle meaning 'untouched/unused/unworn'. به دست is a locative/ instrumental phrase meaning 'in (his) hand' or 'with (his) hand'. Together: 'a certain unworn garment in his hand.'
segments.s323-g45: بپوشید is past 3rd‑person 'he put on / he clothed (himself)'. و is the coordinating 'and'. بنشست is past 3rd‑person 'he sat down'. بر تخت عاج is the locative phrase 'on the ivory throne' (عاج = 'ivory') specifying where he sat. The verbs are simple past, describing successive actions.
segments.s323-g46: جهاندار is an honorific noun 'world‑holder' = 'sovereign/king'. بی‌یاره is an epithet 'without aide/support' i.e. 'friendless/unsupported'. The following و ... و list (گرز و تاج) names his accoutrements: گرز 'mace/club' and تاج 'crown'. The segment strings these descriptive nouns together as apposition to the ruler.
segments.s323-g1: جهاندار is the subject 'the sovereign / world-ruler' followed by the past verb شد 'became/stood'. پیش here is a prepositional adverb 'before, in the presence of' and is placed before the epithet برتر خدای 'the Exalted/Supreme God' (poetic inversion). The clause therefore reads literally 'the sovereign stood before the supreme God.'
segments.s323-g2: همی is the archaic/progressive particle marking continuous or iterative aspect ('was/then'); خواست is a past-tense verb 'wished/asked'. تا introduces a purposive/subjunctive clause ('so that / that'), and باشدش is a subjunctive/optative form 'may it be' with the clitic -ش = 'for him/it'. رهنمای is a predicate noun 'guide, adviser'. Together: 'he wished that He be his guide.'
segments.s323-g3: همی گفت combines the progressive particle همی with the past verb گفت 'said' (a poetic habitual/past narrative). کای is the vocative particle 'O', addressing کردگار سپهر — an inverted nominal phrase meaning 'Maker/Creator of the sky/heavens' (کردگار = Creator, سپهر = the sky/heavens).
segments.s323-g4: فروزنده is a participial/epithetic adjective 'illuminating, radiant' modifying the following abstract nouns. نیکی و داد و مهر are three coordinated nouns ('goodness / good deed', 'justice', 'kindness/affection') joined by و; they are epithets of the Creator invoked in address.
segments.s323-g5: از این شهریاری is a prepositional phrase 'from / of this kingship'; the two tokens م + را here together form the enclitic object مرا 'to/for me' (data splits them). سود نیست is an idiom 'there is no profit / it does not profit'. The whole means 'this kingship brings me no benefit.'
segments.s323-g6: گر is the conditional 'if'. از من here means 'with respect to me / concerning me'. خداوند خشنود نیست is the main clause of the condition 'the Lord is not pleased' (خشنود = pleased; نیست = is not). Together: 'if the Lord is not pleased with me.'
segments.s323-g7: ز من is a poetic variant of از من 'from me'. نیکوی‌گر is an archaic compound meaning roughly 'the doer/thing of good' (here 'good [deeds]'); پذیرفت is a past-form used here in a conditional/hypothetical sense 'accepts' (classical diction). و زشت links the contrasting element 'and the bad/evil' (زشت = evil, ugly) — the speaker contrasts acceptance of good with rejection of bad.
segments.s323-g8: نشستن here is the verbal noun/infinitive 'the sitting / seat' (object of جای ده). م + را again form مرا 'me' (object). جای ده is an imperative or entreaty 'give a place' (جای = place, ده = give). در بهشت is the locative 'in Paradise'. The whole clause is a plea: 'place my seat in Paradise.'
segments.s323-g17: که‌ای is a vocative/exclamatory address (older written as که ای) equivalent to English “O”; it introduces direct address. شاه is the noun 'king' being addressed; نیک‌اختر and نیک‌بخت are epithets in apposition to شاه ('auspicious-star' / 'fortunate'), joined by و. Word order is noun + epithets (apposition) after the vocative particle.
segments.s323-g18: بسودی functions as a predicative phrase meaning 'you will profit/gain' (classical/poetic formation b- + سودی or an archaic verbal form); بسی is an adverb 'much/many' modifying the following noun یاره 'companions/attendants'. The coordinate phrase و تاج و تخت ('and crown and throne') lists further things received; the whole segment states the result/benefit that accrues to the addressee.
segments.s323-g19: اگر marks a conditional 'if'. ز این جهان = 'from this world' (ز is the poetic preposition 'from' + این جهان 'this world'); تیز is an adverb/adjective 'swift(ly)'. بشتافتی is a 2nd-person form 'you hastened/you departed quickly' (an older/poetic past/imperfective used in the protasis of the conditional). The clause sets up a hypothetical/real condition about leaving life.
segments.s323-g20: کنون = 'now' introducing the result clause. آنچه is a free relative 'that which/whatever'; جستی here means 'you sought' (classical sense 'seek'); همه is an adverb/adjective 'all/entirely' modifying یافتی; یافتی is 2nd-person past 'you found/obtained'. The clause asserts that the addressee has already obtained whatever he sought.
segments.s323-g21: به همسیایگی is a prepositional phrase 'in/to the neighborhood/proximity of'; داور پاک is a noun phrase 'the Pure Judge' (داور as divine/judicial figure + پاک 'pure'); جای is the noun 'place' and serves as the thing that will be found. The whole segment is a locative nominal phrase serving as (part of) the object/place for the verb in the next segment.
segments.s323-g22: بیابی is a 2nd-person form meaning 'you will find' (poetic/future or present-subjunctive sense used predictively). بدین is the demonstrative/adverbial 'by/with this' (بدین = 'with/in this'), تیرگی 'darkness' names the negative condition, and در + مپای is a short poetic locution modifying how one stands with respect to that darkness; overall the clause warns about that darkness while asserting the finding of a place. Grammatically, the verb (بیابی) governs the following locative/adverbial phrase.
segments.s323-g23: چو is a temporal/conditional conjunction 'when/if'. بخشی here functions as 'a giving/portion' or a verbalized noun (contextually 'when you give'); به introduces the indirect object (to/for) and ارزانیان is a plural noun referring to the lowly/recipients (those to whom things are granted cheaply/charitably). بخش (repeated) is a noun/verb 'portion/gift' and گنج is the object 'treasure'. Syntactically this is a conditional/temporal clause telling what to do when giving.
segments.s323-g24: کسی را uses را as the direct-object/topic marker with کسی 'someone' (i.e. 'to someone'); سپار is the verb 'entrust/hand over' (imperative/indicative sense here: 'entrust'); این سرای سپنج is a genitive/nominal phrase 'this sponge-like house' (سپنج 'sponge' used metaphorically). Word order is verb after object marker (common in Persian poetry), so the clause instructs to entrust the named house/thing to a person.
segments.s323-g9: چنین is an adverb meaning 'thus/so' modifying the whole phrase. پنج هفته is a numeral+noun 'five weeks' with the adjective خروشان ('roaring/tumultuous') placed after the noun as is normal in Persian poetry. به پای is a prepositional phrase 'at the foot(s) (of)/at the feet (of)' that connects this temporal/locational phrase to the verb in the next segment.
segments.s323-g10: همی + past verb (بود) gives a continuous/iterative past sense ('was / kept on being'); here همی marks ongoing action. بود is the past form 'was'. بر پیش is a compound prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of' and گیهان خدای is a genitive phrase 'God (or Lord) of the worlds' where گیهان (worlds) modifies خدای.
segments.s323-g11: شب تیره is a nominative noun phrase 'the dark night'. از رنج gives the cause 'from/because of suffering' (از used causally). نغنود is a poetical/past form of غنودن with a negative prefix (ن-), here meaning 'did not sleep' (the verb precedes the subject شاه, which follows for emphasis: 'the king did not sleep').
segments.s323-g12: بدانگه is an adverb 'thereupon/at that moment'. که is the conjunction 'when/that' introducing a subordinate time clause. برزد is an archaic past verb meaning 'appeared/issued' (here 'rose/appeared'); the verb comes before its noun سر ('head/top') in poetic inversion. از برج ماه is a prepositional phrase 'from the tower of the moon' (i.e. the moon rose above the tower).
segments.s323-g13: بخفت is a past-tense verb 'slept' (verb before subject, poetic order). او is the pronoun 'he'. و links the clauses; روشن روانش is a noun phrase 'his bright/luminous soul' where -ش is the possessive enclitic. نخفت is the negative past form of خفتن 'to sleep' ('did not sleep') referring to the soul; again verb follows its noun for emphasis.
segments.s323-g14: که here gives an explanatory/casual clause 'for/because'. اندر جهان = 'in the world'. با خرد 'with wisdom' (با + noun). بود is the past copula 'was' and جفت is the predicate meaning 'pair/companion, mate' (i.e. 'he had a counterpart/mate, who was wise'); word order places بود before its predicate for poetic effect.
segments.s323-g15: چنان دید در خواب = 'thus he saw in a dream' (چنان 'thus', دید past 'saw', در خواب 'in sleep/in a dream'). کاو را is an archaic/dialectal element meaning 'to him/whom' (object/dative) marking the person addressed; this is not the verbal root 'to search' here but an archaic pronoun. به گوش 'into the ear' indicates that what was said was whispered/heard in the ear.
segments.s323-g16: نهفته here is an adjective/adverb 'hidden/secret' used adverbially ('secretly'). بگفتی is a poetic past-form 'said/spoke' (verb placed before its subject, a common poetic word order). خجسته سروش is the subject phrase 'auspicious Soroush' (Soroush = divine messenger/angel) and follows the verb for emphasis.
segments.s323-g33: Imperative clause with گزین ('choose/select') as the verb. سر تخت را is marked with را as the definite/direct object (the thing/person to be chosen); پادشاهی is a nominal complement meaning 'kingship/sovereignty' (what the object is to be made). Word order is poetic but follows: [object (سر تخت را)] [complement (پادشاهی)] [verb (گزین)].
segments.s323-g34: Relative clause introduced by که ('who/that') describing the chosen ruler. ایمن بود is past-tense copula + adjective 'was safe/protected'; مور (the ant) is the logical patient/beneficiary and appears after the verb due to poetic inversion; از او ('from him') indicates safety with respect to that person, and بر زمین ('on the ground') gives the location.
segments.s323-g35: Temporal clause begun by چو ('when'). گیتی is the object of ببخشی (2nd-person verb 'you give/bestow'). میاسای is a present-form of 'to rest/settle' used here in the sense of refrain/stop (in context a prohibitive sense with هیچ for emphasis), and هیچ ('at all/none') intensifies the negation: 'do not rest at all.'
segments.s323-g36: Reason/consequence clause introduced by که ('for/that'). آمد is past 'came' (used idiomatically for 'may come' or 'will come'); ت + را together function as the 2nd-person object 'ترا' ('to you'); روزگار is the abstract noun 'fortune/time/fate' that is said to come; بسیچ is an adverbial intensifier 'very/much'. The phrase thus means 'for much fortune may come to you.'
segments.s323-g37: Temporal clause with چو ('when') + compound verb بیدار شد ('became awake/awoke'). رنج‌دیده is an epithet 'the one who had suffered' (a participial adjective) serving as the subject; ز خواب is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from sleep' indicating the source/state before waking.
segments.s323-g38: ز خوی دید is a short clause meaning 'he saw, by/through his habit/disposition' (ز = 'by/from', خوی = 'disposition/habit', دید = 'he saw'). جای پرستش is a noun phrase 'a place of worship'; پر آب is an adjectival phrase 'full of water' (پر = 'full (of)') placed after the noun in poetic word order.
segments.s323-g39: Progressive/past continuous sense with همی بود ('he was continually/was in the state of') + گریان ('weeping') as the predicative adjective/participle. و رخ بر زمین links another clause describing posture: 'and (his) face upon the ground' — رخ = 'face', بر زمین = 'on the ground.'
segments.s323-g40: همی خواند is iterative/continuous past 'he kept reciting/calling.' بر here means 'to/toward' (directing the utterance), کردگار = 'the Creator/God', and آفرین is an acclamation/praise (an interjection 'praise/hallelujah') addressed to God. The clause reports his continual vocal praise directed at the Creator.
segments.s323-g25: This chunk contains the conditional protasis: توانگر شوی = 'you become/become rich' with شوی the 2nd‑person singular present/subjunctive of شدن used here as a future/result; گر is the conditional particle 'if' attached (printed here as شوی‌گر). The subject تو appears after that (typical Persian flexibility) and درویش را is a fronted definite object marked by را — it will be the object of the verb in the next segment.
segments.s323-g26: کنی is the present/subjunctive of کردن 'to make/cause' used here in the conditional clause started by گر (so: 'if you make...'); شادمان is an adjective 'happy', while مردم خویش را is the definite object 'your people' (خویش is split into خوی + ش where ش is the enclitic possessive 'his/her/its' used here as 'your' after تو). The object phrase precedes the verb, but کنی governs شادمان and مردم خویش را.
segments.s323-g27: کسی here acts as an indefinite/relative 'whoever' and گردد (from گشتن) is the present/subjunctive 'becomes/is made' giving 'becomes safe'; ایمن is the predicate adjective 'safe'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from', and چنگ بلا is a genitive-like phrase 'the claw/hold of calamity' (چنگ = claw/talon, بلا = calamity). Word order places the verb before the subject and the prepositional phrase after the predicate.
segments.s323-g28: که introduces a relative/subordinate clause ('who/that'); یابد (from یافتن) is a present/subjunctive/optative 'finds/attains'; رها here functions as a noun/adjective 'release / freed' that is the object/complement of یابد. ز marks 'from', این دم اژدها = 'this breath (or mouth/edge) of the dragon' (a poetic image for deadly danger).
segments.s323-g29: هرآنکس 'whoever' begins the relative clause; که is the subordinator; از بهر تو = 'for your sake' (از بهر is a fixed prepositional phrase). رنج برد is a compound verb meaning 'to bear/endure suffering' (رنج = hardship/suffering, برد here is the verb 'to bear' in context). The verb is past/perfect in sense — 'has borne' or more generally 'endures/undergoes' in poetry.
segments.s323-g30: چنان دان is an imperative/imperative‑like instruction: دان (from دانستن) here means 'consider/regard [him] so'. که introduces the explanatory clause: آن 'that person' + از پی گنج 'in pursuit of treasure' (پی = 'after/in pursuit of') and برد here completes the clause — in sense 'suffered/searched/strived' so the whole means 'consider him as if he had sought treasure'.
segments.s323-g31: چو = 'when/if'; بخشی here functions as the verb 'you give' (classical Persian present/subjunctive form) or nominalized 'a giving' depending on reading; به ارزانیان = 'to the humble/lowly' (ارزانیان = recipients of cheap/generous gifts in context). بخش چیز is an instruction 'give a thing/something' — the verb/object sequence, with بخش as 'give' or 'a gift/portion' and چیز = 'thing'.
segments.s323-g32: که introduces a purpose/result clause here ('so that'); ایدر = 'here'; نمانی is the negative-present/subjunctive of ماندن 'you should not remain / be left' (ن- negative prefix + مانی); تو is the emphatic subject pronoun; بسیار نیز = 'much/very (beyond this) also' i.e. 'not be left here with much lacking either'. The clause gives the intended effect of the giving.

sentences.s323-s21: He kept saying, "If I had hurried swiftly, I would have obtained from God every desire of my heart."
sentences.s323-s22: He came and sat upon the royal throne; a certain unworn garment in his hand.
sentences.s323-s23: He put it on and sat on the ivory throne, the sovereign—alone and friendless—with mace and crown.
sentences.s323-s1: The sovereign stood before the Most High God and asked that He be his guide.
sentences.s323-s2: He then said, 'O Maker of the heavens, illuminator of goodness, justice and kindness.'
sentences.s323-s3: This kingship profits me nothing, if the Lord is not pleased with me.
sentences.s323-s4: If He accepts the good from me and [rejects] the bad, give me a place to sit in Paradise.
sentences.s323-s9: O king of auspicious star and good fortune, you shall gain great advantage: many companions, and crown and throne.
sentences.s323-s10: If you hastened swiftly from this world, now whatever you sought you have wholly obtained.
sentences.s323-s11: You will find a place beside the Pure Judge; do not linger in this darkness.
sentences.s323-s12: When you give, give (a portion) to the lowly; entrust this spongy house to someone.
sentences.s323-s5: Thus for five tumultuous weeks he remained at the feet before the Lord of the worlds.
sentences.s323-s6: The dark night, because of anguish, did not let the king sleep until the moment when the moon's head rose above the tower.
sentences.s323-s7: He slept, but his bright (luminous) soul did not sleep, for in the world his mate was one of wisdom.
sentences.s323-s8: Thus he saw in a dream that the auspicious Soroush secretly spoke into his ear.
sentences.s323-s17: Choose a ruler for the head of the throne — one under whom even an ant on the earth would be safe.
sentences.s323-s18: When you bestow the world, do not rest at all, for great fortune may come to you.
sentences.s323-s19: When the one who had suffered awoke from sleep, by habit he saw a place of worship full of water.
sentences.s323-s20: He was weeping with his face on the ground, and he kept calling out 'Praise be to the Creator.'
sentences.s323-s13: If you make the dervish (poor man) happy, you will become wealthy and make your own people glad.
sentences.s323-s14: Whoever is saved from the claw of calamity is freed from this dragon's breath.
sentences.s323-s15: Whoever endures hardship for your sake, consider him as if he endured it in pursuit of treasure.
sentences.s323-s16: When you give to the humble, give something so that you yourself will not be left poor here as well.
